rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Scanned for: honest, delve, landscape (verb), importantly, crucial, substrate, leverage (verb), synergize, paradigm shift. No matches found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban in any curriculum or training surface.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No instances of 'practice' (noun), 'ritual', or 'ceremony' in body (lines 1-53). 'practice of risk' in maintainer note (line 64) is exempt — below divider.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No '[NOTE: outdated]', 'retained as historical', 'compounded YYYY-MM-DD', 'item origins' tables, or dated log markers in body. Maintainer block correctly carries QA metadata below divider.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match. AE101 voice quintet + Agents 101 voice trio.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Register is student-facing, concrete, and warm. Voice matches Agents 101 trio (Godin peer warmth + Sutherland reframe + Siilasmaa action): 'Not magic' (Godin), 'the reusable check will be' (Sutherland-framing), 'The check carries the expertise you fed it, and you author it' (Siilasmaa forward-action). No L&D-coach register, no motivational-poster tone.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No recognizable techniques paraphrased without credit. References STRIDE and ISO 31000 / NIST in maintainer notes (lines 60-62), correctly placed below divider. No practitioner moves embedded in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector — reassurance prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No reassurance phrases like 'no penalty', 'fine way to', 'it's OK if', 'not a downgrade'. Prose is direct: 'The check carries the expertise', 'You will not need to be', established with confidence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No vendor defensiveness, no 'not aspirational', no 'unlike consultancy decks', no 'real practitioners' framing. Body teaches mechanism (skill scope, layered security, residual-risk cadence) without importing positioning posture. Boundary vocabulary (policy-lens vs agent-risk-lens) earns in context, not as positioning claim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 35: 'SKILL.md' and 'attack class' appear in operational section (Reading 2) before earning in body. Line 35 'prompt injection' (direct + indirect) uses jargon that gains real definition 4 lines later. Line 32 'non-deterministic' is an LLM-internal term that hasn't been earned by this point in the module arc. Early plantings pre-spoil future teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 30–35: swap 'prompt injection' for 'the agent deciding to do something harmful because of what it read' (plain mechanic). Defer 'attack class' vocabulary to Module 5 or add one-sentence earn inline: 'These are named attack patterns, part of your threat model like classical software has threat vectors.' Line 32 'non-determinism' → 'different outputs on different runs' (plain). Line 35 'attack class' → 'risk pattern'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No combative verbs (bark, order, command, force, drag, push, boss, shout, snap, lecture, scold). Verbs used: 'loads', 'decides', 'retrieves', 'runs', 'can reach'. All peer-flat or collaborative.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module names what the student is becoming ('you will first run your company's policy files raw, then package the useful check'). No deficit framing like 'you're missing' or 'you already think'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 15 'policy lens' and 'agent-risk lens' used operationally before definition. Line 35 'scope', 'split', 'filter', 'gate', 'review' introduced as mitigation shapes in a list format without context that this is a named taxonomy students will reuse. Line 11 'SKILL.md' used without one-breath primer at first appearance.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 11: earn 'SKILL.md' — 'A named bundle of expertise (a file called SKILL.md plus supporting files) that the agent loads when relevant.' Line 15: shift 'policy lens' and 'agent-risk lens' to inline description before the term: 'two lenses: one reading your company's rules (policy lens), one reading what the agent can reach and leak (agent-risk lens).' Line 35 'scope, split, filter, gate, review' — add a lead sentence: 'New mitigations sit above classical controls: give the agent less (scope), break one agent into two (split), post-process outputs (filter), require a human check (gate), or have another agent judge the first (review).'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.evidence: No unearned tech words. Terminology used ('non-deterministic', 'residual risk', 'threat model', 'credentials') are either earned in context or belong to the discipline being taught. No SVP-facing flinches detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 13: 'Module 4's first exercise walks you through authoring one in your runtime' — correctly uses 'runtime' not 'memory' (runtime = session state). No conflation detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No em-dashes found. Sentences are short and direct throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Not applicable — this is a prework reading (lecture surface), not an exercise. Prose is declarative-narrative-appropriate, not action-imperative.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No inline links in body. Maintainer references are bare text or paths (acceptable below divider).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Vocabulary correctly deployed: 'the agent' when describing execution/action ('the agent loads', 'the agent retrieves and reads', 'the agent might follow'), 'Claude' when naming addressee ('Ask Claude to'). No conflation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'tomorrow', 'overnight', 'this morning', 'tonight', 'yesterday', 'last night' found in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module 4 mood (per maintainer notes line 63) is 'deepened unease' / 'sustained unease'. Body delivers: 'Safe enough, most of the time, within named limits' (uncertainty), layers of new attack classes, cadence acceleration. Mood is honoured, not flattened.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Unease is framed as *not-knowing* ('residual fluctuates rather than settling', 'cannot fully predict it'), not as failure or shame. Student leaves uncertain about what's possible, not ashamed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Reading 1 names 'personal skills' and 'reusable expertise' (discipline). Reading 2 names 'layered controls' and 'residual risk' (discipline), with failure modes (hallucination, injection, secrets leaks) as the negative cases the discipline addresses.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — cross-module sequencing claims.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework reading frames Module 4 as first exposure to security layering + personal skills authoring. No downstream-module teaching moments are front-run (Module 5 complexity escalations, Module 6 synthesis patterns left intact).
